questions.question: Which ancient Indian text is considered the earliest known work of Indian philosophy and spirituality?
questions.explanation: The Vedas are the oldest texts in Indian history, dating back to around 1500 BCE, and are fundamental to Indian philosophy and spirituality.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in India in 1526 after his victory at the Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: Which movement is associated with the Non-Cooperation Movement in India?
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920 as a mass protest against British rule, emphasizing non-cooperation with colonial authorities.

questions.question: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred in which year?
questions.explanation: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre took place in 1919 when British troops killed hundreds of unarmed civilians protesting colonial policies.

questions.question: Who was the first President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India, serving from 1950 to 1962.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari is the longest river in India, flowing about 1,465 km across central India.

questions.question: The Thar Desert is located primarily in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is primarily located in Rajasthan, covering a significant part of the state.

questions.question: Which resource is India most dependent on importation?
questions.explanation: India heavily relies on imported crude oil to meet its energy needs.

questions.question: Which Himalayan river is known for creating the Ganga-Brahmaputra delta?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra River, along with the Ganges, forms the world's largest delta in the Bengal basin.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Justice of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: As of 2026, Surya Kant is serving as the Chief Justice of India.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 12 to 35 of the Indian Constitution deal with Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.question: Which Panchayati Raj institution is directly elected by villagers?
questions.explanation: The Gram Sabha, comprising villagers, is directly elected and functions as the primary unit of local governance.

questions.question: The power to declare a state of emergency in India is vested in which authority?
questions.explanation: The President, on the advice of the Union Cabinet, can declare a national or state emergency.

questions.question: India's GDP is primarily contributed by which sector?
questions.explanation: The Services sector contributes the largest share to India's GDP among all sectors.

questions.question: The 'Make in India' initiative was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: 'Make in India' was launched by the Government of India in 2014 to promote manufacturing.

questions.question: Which institution is responsible for framing India’s monetary policy?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) formulates and implements India’s monetary policy.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Goods and Services Tax (GST)?
questions.explanation: GST aims to unify and simplify indirect taxation across India to create a single national market.

questions.question: Which law explains the phenomenon of refraction of light?
questions.explanation: Snell's Law explains how light bends when passing from one medium to another.

questions.question: Which element is used as a moderator in nuclear reactors?
questions.explanation: Graphite is commonly used as a moderator in nuclear reactors to slow down neutrons.

questions.question: The process of photosynthesis produces which of the following?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into oxygen and glucose, releasing oxygen.

questions.question: Which space agency launched the Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan)?
questions.explanation: ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) launched Mangalyaan in 2013.

questions.question: Which of the following is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in India?
questions.explanation: Kaziranga National Park is designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site for its biodiversity.

questions.question: The Kyoto Protocol deals with which environmental issue?
questions.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

questions.question: Which national park is famous for the Bengal tiger in India?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans is renowned for the Bengal tiger and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Which law in India regulates environmental protection?
questions.explanation: The Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, provides a comprehensive framework for environmental regulation.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been serving as the President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been serving as Prime Minister since 26 May 2014.

questions.question: Which international organization was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2025?
questions.explanation: The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) received the Nobel Peace Prize in 2025 for its work on climate change.

questions.question: Which national scheme aims to improve sanitation and eradicate open defecation?
questions.explanation: Swachh Bharat Abhiyan was launched to improve sanitation and eliminate open defecation in India.
